title: 10 best herbal teas for hangover
intro_desc: Herbal teas for hangover are infusions made from various plants and herbs that help alleviate the symptoms of a hangover. These teas typically contain ingredients like ginger, lemon balm, chamomile, and milk thistle, which are known for their anti-inflammatory, calming, and liver-detoxifying properties. By drinking these teas, individuals can reduce the severity of hangover symptoms such as headaches, nausea, and dehydration. For example, ginger tea can help soothe an upset stomach, while chamomile tea can promote relaxation and improve sleep quality. By incorporating these teas into their routine, individuals can improve their overall well-being and reduce the negative effects of alcohol consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wormwood tea helps with hangover because it contains a compound called thujone, which is believed to stimulate the digestive system and promote the metabolism of alcohol in the body. This can help to speed up the detoxification process and alleviate hangover symptoms such as nausea, headache, and fatigue. Additionally, wormwood tea is known for its anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce inflammation in the body and further ease hangover discomfort. Furthermore, wormwood tea can help to rehydrate the body, which is essential for recovering from a hangover.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Wormwood tea helps reduce inflammation in the body, which can help alleviate hangover symptoms such as headaches and nausea, thanks to compounds like beta-thujone and camphene.

Antioxidant: The tea's antioxidant properties help combat oxidative stress caused by alcohol consumption, protecting liver function and aiding in overall recovery, due to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids.

Digestive stimulant: Wormwood tea promotes digestion and can help alleviate stomach issues related to hangovers, as it contains absinthin and anabsinthin which stimulate the production of digestive enzymes.

Diuretic: The tea's diuretic properties help eliminate toxins from the body, aiding in the detoxification process and reducing the severity of hangover symptoms, thanks to the lactone sesquiterpenes, including artabsin.

Anxiolytic: Wormwood tea may help reduce anxiety and promote relaxation, which can be beneficial during a hangover, due to the presence of compounds like chamazulene and matricin which have calming effects on the central nervous system.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, boil 1 cup of water for every 1-2 teaspoons of dried wormwood herb.
Reduce heat, add wormwood to pot, and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove wormwood herb pieces.
Allow the tea to cool down to a drinkable temperature.
Drink the wormwood tea slowly, sipping as needed for hangover relief.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in medicinal compounds, the leaves are used to make teas that help alleviate hangover symptoms.
Seeds: Containing essential oils, the seeds are used in teas to aid in digestion and reduce inflammation caused by hangovers.
Flowers: With potent antioxidants, the flowers are used in teas to help detoxify the body and speed up recovery from a hangover.
Bark: Known for its calming properties, the bark is used in teas to soothe nerves and reduce anxiety associated with hangovers.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion tea helps with hangover because it is a natural diuretic that can help flush out toxins from the body, including those that contribute to a hangover. It is also rich in vitamins and minerals, such as vitamin C, potassium, and magnesium, which can help replenish nutrients lost during alcohol consumption. Additionally, dandelion tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation and alleviate some of the symptoms associated with a hangover, such as headaches and nausea. Overall, drinking dandelion tea can help rehydrate the body, restore nutrients, and reduce inflammation, making it a great natural remedy for hangovers.
remedy_properties: Diuretic: Dandelion tea increases urine production, helping to eliminate toxins and reduce fluid retention, which can alleviate hangover symptoms. This is due to the presence of taraxacin, a potent diuretic found in dandelion.

Anti-inflammatory: Dandelion tea can help reduce inflammation and swelling, which may be caused by alcohol consumption. The anti-inflammatory properties come from the sesquiterpene lactones, such as germacranolide and eudesmanolide, present in dandelion.

Antioxidant: Dandelion tea contains high levels of antioxidants, which help protect cells from damage caused by free radicals, including those generated during alcohol metabolism. The phenolic acids, flavonoids, and carotenoids in dandelion contribute to its antioxidant properties.

Hepatoprotective: Dandelion tea supports liver function and helps to detoxify the body, which can help alleviate hangover symptoms. This is due to the presence of taraxasterol, a compound known for its hepatoprotective effects.

Nutrient-rich: Dandelion tea is a rich source of vitamins and minerals, including vitamin A, C, and K, as well as potassium, calcium, and iron. These nutrients can help replenish the body and support overall health, which can aid in hangover recovery.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2-3 tablespoons of fresh dandelion flowers or 1-2 teaspoons of dried dandelion flowers.
Rinse the flowers thoroughly under cold water to remove any dirt or debris.
Place the flowers in a teapot and pour 1 cup of boiling water over them.
Allow the tea to steep for 5-10 minutes, then strain out the flowers.
Drink the tea slowly, enjoying its earthy flavor, and repeat as needed for hangover relief.
remedy_parts: Roots: Dandelion roots are often used to make medicinal teas for hangovers due to their ability to support liver function and help the body eliminate toxins.
Leaves: Dandelion leaves are rich in vitamins and minerals, making them a popular choice for teas that can help replenish nutrients lost during heavy drinking.
Flowers: Dandelion flowers are used in teas for their anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate headaches and other hangover symptoms.
Buds: Dandelion buds contain high levels of antioxidants, which can help protect the body from damage caused by alcohol consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: German chamomile tea helps with hangover because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce the inflammation and irritation in the stomach and intestines caused by excessive alcohol consumption. It also contains calming compounds that can help alleviate symptoms such as anxiety, irritability, and restlessness. Furthermore, chamomile tea is a natural diuretic, which can help flush toxins out of the body and rehydrate the system, helping to restore balance and alleviate hangover symptoms. Additionally, chamomile tea can help promote better sleep, which is essential for the body to recover from the effects of alcohol.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: German chamomile tea contains active constituents like bisabolol and matricin, which are converted to chamazulene, that help reduce inflammation in the body, thereby alleviating hangover-induced headaches and inflammation.
Antioxidant: The high antioxidant content, including flavonoids and terpenoids, helps combat oxidative stress caused by alcohol consumption, protecting cells and accelerating alcohol metabolism.
Diuretic: German chamomile encourages diuresis, increasing urine production, which aids in flushing out toxins and eliminating byproducts of alcohol metabolism, thus speeding up recovery.
Nervine: Chamomile has calming properties due to its volatile oils, such as apigenin and luteolin, which help soothe the nervous system, reducing anxiety and promoting better sleep during a hangover.

Carminative: German chamomile tea's carminative properties, derived from its essential oils, help relax the gastrointestinal muscles, alleviating nausea, bloating, and abdominal discomfort often associated with hangovers.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 2 teaspoons of dried German chamomile flowers and place them in a tea infuser or teapot.
Boil 8 ounces of water and let it cool slightly for 1-2 minutes.
Pour the hot water over the chamomile flowers and let them steep for 5-10 minutes.
Remove the tea infuser or strain out the chamomile flowers.
Drink the tea slowly, allowing it to soothe your stomach and help with hangover symptoms.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers of German chamomile are rich in flavonoids and essential oils, which have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that can help alleviate hangover symptoms such as nausea, headache, and stomach discomfort.
Leaves: The leaves of German chamomile contain tannins and volatile oils that can help soothe an upset stomach and promote relaxation, making them useful in hangover tea.

Seeds: German chamomile seeds are a good source of essential oils, including chamazulene, which has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce hangover symptoms such as inflammation and oxidative stress.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint tea helps with hangover because it contains menthol, a compound that can soothe and relax the stomach muscles, thereby reducing the symptoms of nausea and vomiting that often accompany a hangover. Additionally, peppermint tea can help to alleviate headaches and promote healthy digestion, which can be disrupted by excessive alcohol consumption. The hydrating effects of the tea can also help to replace fluids lost during a night of drinking, further aiding in the recovery process. Ultimately, the soothing and restorative properties of peppermint tea make it an ideal choice for those seeking relief from the unpleasant symptoms of a hangover.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Peppermint tea helps reduce inflammation in the body, including the inflammation caused by a hangover, due to the presence of menthol and flavonoids.
Antiemetic: Peppermint tea can help alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with hangovers, thanks to the menthol and methyl salicylate content.
Diuretic: Peppermint tea promotes diuresis, helping the body eliminate toxins and alleviating hangover symptoms, due to its volatile oils.
Antioxidant: Peppermint tea contains antioxidants that help combat oxidative stress and cell damage caused by alcohol consumption, thanks to the rosmarinic acid and various flavonoids.
Analgesic: Peppermint tea can help relieve headaches and muscle pain associated with hangovers, due to the presence of menthol, rosmarinic acid, and flavonoids.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 1 teaspoon of dried peppermint leaves or 2 teaspoons of fresh peppermint leaves.
Place the peppermint leaves in a tea infuser or teapot.
Pour boiling water over the peppermint leaves and let them steep for 5-10 minutes.
Remove the tea infuser or strain the tea into a cup.
Drink the peppermint tea while it is still warm, up to three times a day as needed.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in menthol and menthone, peppermint leaves are used in medicinal teas for hangover due to their ability to soothe digestive issues and reduce nausea.
Flowers: Containing essential oils and antioxidants, peppermint flowers are used in medicinal teas for hangover to provide anti-inflammatory and calming effects on the body.
Seeds: High in antioxidants and volatile oils, peppermint seeds are used in medicinal teas for hangover to support liver function and promote detoxification.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm tea helps with hangover because it has calming and anti-inflammatory properties that can alleviate some of the unpleasant symptoms associated with a hangover. The soothing effect of lemon balm can help reduce anxiety and agitation, while its anti-inflammatory properties can help alleviate headaches and muscle aches. Additionally, lemon balm has been shown to promote sleep and improve mood, which can help combat fatigue and irritability. Furthermore, lemon balm contains antioxidants that can help eliminate toxins from the body, including those that contribute to a hangover. Drinking lemon balm tea can help rehydrate the body, which is essential for recovering from a night of drinking. Overall, lemon balm tea can provide a natural and effective way to manage hangover symptoms and promote recovery.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Lemon balm tea helps reduce inflammation in the body, which can help alleviate hangover symptoms such as headaches and nausea, thanks to its rosmarinic acid content.
Antioxidant: The high antioxidant content of lemon balm tea, including rosmarinic acid and caffeic acid, helps combat oxidative stress caused by alcohol consumption, aiding in hangover recovery.
Nervine: Lemon balm tea has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help with anxiety and restlessness often associated with hangovers, due to its content of rosmarinic acid and eugenol.
Diaphoretic: Lemon balm tea promotes sweating, helping the body eliminate toxins and rehydrate, which is crucial for hangover relief, due to its volatile oils and flavonoids.
Carminative: Lemon balm tea can help soothe the digestive system and reduce gastric inflammation, alleviating hangover-related stomach issues, thanks to its rosmarinic acid and eugenol content.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 1-2 teaspoons of dried lemon balm leaves and place them in a tea infuser or loose in a teapot.
Boil water and let it cool slightly for 1-2 minutes before pouring it over the lemon balm leaves.
Allow the tea to steep for 5-7 minutes to extract the beneficial compounds from the herb.
Remove the tea infuser or strain the tea into a cup to remove the leaves.
Optionally, add honey or lemon to taste, and drink the tea while it's still warm to help alleviate hangover symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of lemon balm are commonly used in medicinal teas for hangovers due to their high concentration of antioxidants and soothing properties, which can help to alleviate symptoms such as nausea and headaches.

Flowers: The flowers of lemon balm contain volatile oils and flavonoids that have been shown to have calming effects on the nervous system, making them useful in medicinal teas for hangovers to help reduce anxiety and promote relaxation.

Stems: While not as potent as the leaves or flowers, the stems of lemon balm can still contribute to the medicinal properties of teas for hangovers, as they contain trace amounts of antioxidants and other beneficial compounds.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel tea helps with hangover because it contains properties that can help the body recover from the effects of alcohol. Fennel is known for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the liver and other organs that are affected by alcohol consumption. Additionally, fennel tea can help rehydrate the body, which is essential after a night of drinking, as alcohol can cause dehydration and electrolyte imbalances. The calming and soothing effects of fennel tea may also help alleviate symptoms such as nausea, headache, and upset stomach that are commonly associated with a hangover.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Fennel tea helps reduce inflammation in the body, which can help alleviate hangover symptoms such as headaches and nausea, due to its active constituents like volatile oils, flavonoids and phenolic acids.

Diuretic: Fennel tea promotes diuresis, helping the body eliminate toxins and reduce fluid retention, which can help relieve hangover-related symptoms like bloating and sluggishness, due to its active constituent anethole.

Antioxidant: Fennel tea has antioxidant properties that help protect the liver from damage caused by alcohol consumption, which can help speed up the recovery process, due to its active constituents including rosmarinic acid and various flavonoids.

Carminative: Fennel tea helps reduce flatulence and bloating, which can help alleviate hangover symptoms such as abdominal pain and discomfort, due to its active constituents like volatile oils, particularly fenchone and anethole.

Sedative: Fennel tea has a calming effect on the body, which can help improve sleep quality and reduce anxiety, both of which can be affected by heavy alcohol consumption, due to its active constituents like volatile oils and flavonoids.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 1 teaspoon of crushed fennel seeds.
Boil 1 cup of water and add the fennel seeds, letting them steep for 10 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the fennel seeds.
Allow the tea to cool down to a drinkable temperature.
Drink the tea slowly, sipping it gently to soothe your stomach.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Fennel seeds are rich in vitamins and minerals, making them effective in reducing hangover symptoms by rehydrating the body and soothing the stomach.
Leaves: Fennel leaves have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate headaches and muscle cramps caused by hangovers.
Stems: The stems of fennel contain compounds that promote digestion and can help reduce nausea and bloating associated with hangovers.
Flowers: Fennel flowers have antioxidant properties that can help detoxify the liver and reduce the severity of hangover symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tea helps with hangover because it contains compounds that may aid in liver function and detoxification. The tea's antioxidant properties can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which are often byproducts of alcohol consumption. Additionally, licorice root has been shown to help balance cortisol levels, a hormone that can become imbalanced due to excessive alcohol intake. Furthermore, licorice tea can help replenish fluids and electrolytes lost during a night of drinking, providing hydration and supporting overall recovery.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Licorice tea's anti-inflammatory properties, due to active constituents like glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, help reduce inflammation in the body and alleviate hangover symptoms.
Hepatoprotective: Licorice tea's ability to protect the liver, owing to its antioxidant properties, helps counteract alcohol-induced liver damage and support recovery from a hangover.
Adaptogenic: Licorice tea's adaptogenic properties, attributed to active compounds like glycyrrhizin and liquiritin, help the body cope with stress and fatigue, common symptoms of a hangover.
Diuretic: Licorice tea's diuretic properties, resulting from the presence of glycyrrhizic acid, encourage fluid excretion and help eliminate toxins, alleviating hangover-related symptoms.

Gastric mucosal protective: Licorice tea's gastric mucosal protective properties, due to the presence of glycyrrhizin, help maintain the stomach lining and reduce irritation, providing relief from hangover-induced nausea and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, boil 1 cup of water for 3-5 minutes.
Add 1 teaspoon of dried licorice root and reduce heat, simmering for 10-15 minutes.
Remove from heat, let it steep for 5-10 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the solids.
Drink the tea slowly, allowing it to soothe your hangover.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant are commonly used to make medicinal teas for hangovers due to their high concentration of glycyrrhizin, which helps to protect the liver and support the adrenal glands.
Seeds: Licorice seeds are used in medicinal teas for hangovers because they help to soothe digestion, reduce nausea, and alleviate headaches.
Bark: The bark of the licorice plant is used in medicinal teas for hangovers because it helps to detoxify the body, reduce inflammation, and support the immune system.
Leaves: Licorice leaves are used in medicinal teas for hangovers because they contain flavonoids and coumarins, which help to protect the liver and support the cardiovascular system.
Flowers: Licorice flowers are used in medicinal teas for hangovers because they have a calming effect on the nervous system, help to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger tea helps with hangover because it contains properties that can alleviate nausea, vomiting, and inflammation, which are common symptoms of a hangover. Ginger has been used as a natural remedy for centuries to soothe digestive issues, and it can help settle an upset stomach caused by excessive alcohol consumption. Additionally, ginger tea can help rehydrate the body and restore essential minerals and electrolytes lost during a night of drinking. The antioxidants present in ginger tea can also help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation caused by alcohol, further aiding in the recovery process. Overall, ginger tea is a natural and effective way to ease hangover symptoms and promote a faster recovery.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Ginger tea's anti-inflammatory properties, due to compounds like gingerols and shogaols, can help reduce inflammation in the body caused by alcohol consumption, thereby alleviating hangover symptoms.
Antiemetic: Ginger tea's antiemetic effects, attributed to 6-gingerol and 6-shogaol, help alleviate nausea and vomiting, common hangover symptoms.
Diaphoretic: Ginger tea's diaphoretic properties, caused by its essential oils, promote sweating and help the body eliminate toxins, aiding in hangover recovery.
Carminative: Ginger tea's carminative effects, due to its volatile oils, help relax the gastrointestinal tract, reducing abdominal discomfort and bloating associated with hangovers.

Antioxidant: Ginger tea's high antioxidant content, provided by compounds like gingerols, shogaols, and paradols, helps combat oxidative stress caused by alcohol consumption, supporting the body's detoxification process and reducing hangover severity.
remedy_recipe: Peel and chop 1-2 inches of fresh ginger root, allowing 10 minutes for preparation.
Bring 4 cups of water to a boil, then add the chopped ginger, reducing heat and simmering for 15-20 minutes.
Remove from heat, and optionally add 1 cinnamon stick and 1 teaspoon of honey for added flavor and potential benefits.
Steep for 5-10 minutes, then strain the tea to remove ginger pieces.
Sip the warm ginger tea slowly, allowing it to soothe your stomach and potentially alleviate hangover symptoms.
remedy_parts: Rhizomes: Ginger rhizomes are widely used to make medicinal teas for hangovers due to their potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help alleviate nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain.
Leaves: Ginger leaves are used in teas to soothe digestive issues and reduce inflammation, providing relief from hangover symptoms like stomach discomfort and headaches.
Flowers: Ginger flowers contain beneficial compounds that help detoxify the body and support liver function, making them useful in teas to treat hangovers.
Seeds: Ginger seeds are rich in essential oils and offer carminative, expectorant, and antibacterial properties, which can help alleviate hangover symptoms when used in medicinal teas.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eureka tea helps with hangover because it is made with a blend of herbs that are known to support liver function and help the body detoxify. The ingredients in Eureka tea, such as milk thistle, dandelion, and ginger, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress caused by alcohol consumption. Additionally, Eureka tea can help rehydrate the body and replenish essential electrolytes lost during a night of drinking, which can help alleviate hangover symptoms such as headache, nausea, and fatigue. The soothing and calming effects of Eureka tea can also help relax the mind and promote a sense of well-being, making it an ideal remedy for a hangover.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Eureka tea's anti-inflammatory properties, due to the presence of catechins and flavonoids, help reduce inflammation in the body caused by alcohol consumption, thus alleviating hangover symptoms.

Antioxidant: The high antioxidant content, primarily from catechins and tannins, helps neutralize free radicals produced during alcohol metabolism, reducing oxidative stress and damage to cells, thereby helping to combat hangover.

Diuretic: Eureka tea's diuretic properties, attributed to its caffeine content, increase urine production, promoting the elimination of toxins and reducing fluid retention, which can help alleviate hangover symptoms.

Liver support: Eureka tea's liver-protective properties, due to its catechin content, aid in the regeneration of liver cells and protect against alcohol-induced liver damage, which can help speed up recovery from a hangover.

Nervous system support: The soothing effects of Eureka tea on the nervous system, thanks to its theanine content, can help alleviate anxiety, restlessness, and insomnia associated with hangovers, promoting relaxation and better sleep.
remedy_recipe: Chop one inch of ginger root and two lemons into small pieces.
Combine five cups of water, chopped ginger, and lemons in a pot, bringing it to a boil.
Reduce heat, let it simmer for 15 minutes, then remove from heat.
Add one tablespoon of dried rosemary and one tablespoon of dried peppermint leaves, steeping for 10 minutes.
Strain the tea, then add honey or lemon juice to taste before drinking.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the Eureka plant are rich in antioxidants that help to detoxify the liver and reduce the symptoms of a hangover.
Rhizomes: The rhizomes of the Eureka plant contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help to reduce headaches and inflammation associated with hangovers.
Flowers: The flowers of the Eureka plant have calming properties that help to alleviate anxiety and promote relaxation, which can be beneficial for those suffering from a hangover.
Seeds: The seeds of the Eureka plant are a good source of essential fatty acids that help to support liver function and reduce the symptoms of a hangover.
Bark: The bark of the Eureka plant contains tannins that help to soothe an upset stomach and reduce nausea associated with hangovers.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: White tea helps with hangover because it is rich in antioxidants that can help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the liver, which can be caused by excessive alcohol consumption. It also contains caffeine, which can help to alleviate fatigue and headaches that are often associated with hangovers. Additionally, white tea has been shown to help improve blood flow and circulation, which can help to speed up the metabolism and elimination of alcohol from the body. Furthermore, white tea is a natural diuretic, which can help to rehydrate the body and replace lost electrolytes, thus reducing the symptoms of a hangover.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: White tea contains antioxidants called catechins, which help reduce inflammation in the body and alleviate hangover symptoms such as headaches and nausea.
Antioxidant: White tea is rich in polyphenols, which have antioxidant properties that help the body eliminate toxins, such as alcohol, and reduce oxidative stress caused by hangovers.
Diuretic: White tea has a mild diuretic effect due to its caffeine content, which helps the body eliminate excess fluids and toxins, reducing hangover symptoms such as dehydration and nausea.
Antiemetic: White tea contains tannins, which have antiemetic properties that can help alleviate nausea and vomiting caused by hangovers.

Immune support: White tea contains high levels of vitamin C and other antioxidants, which help support the immune system and protect the body from the damaging effects of alcohol, helping to reduce the severity of hangovers.
remedy_recipe: Boil 4 cups of water and let it cool to 175-185°F, as boiling water can damage the delicate white tea leaves.
In a teapot, place 1-2 teaspoons of high-quality white tea leaves, preferably a blend with herbs known to help with hangovers, such as ginger, lemon balm, and chamomile.
Pour the hot water over the tea leaves and let them steep for 3-5 minutes, depending on the desired strength.
Strain the tea into a cup and add honey or lemon to taste, if desired.
Drink the tea slowly, allowing it to soothe your stomach and rehydrate your body. Repeat as needed.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain high levels of antioxidants that help break down alcohol toxins and reduce inflammation.
Buds: Rich in catechins and amino acids, which can help alleviate symptoms of hangover by protecting the liver and replenishing lost nutrients.
Stems: Contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation caused by alcohol consumption.
Roots: Have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe a hangover by reducing symptoms such as nausea, headaches, and irritability.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat a hangover with herbal teas is by consuming calming blends of chamomile and lemon balm, which soothe the stomach and promote relaxation. Ginger tea is excellent for alleviating nausea, while peppermint tea helps reduce inflammation and improves digestion. Additionally, milk thistle tea can help support liver function and detoxification. Staying hydrated with these herbal teas can replenish essential fluids and electrolytes, ultimately easing hangover symptoms.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to hangover that are treated with herbal teas include digestive issues, dehydration, and mild anxiety or stress. Peppermint tea can soothe an upset stomach, while chamomile tea can help with relaxation and promoting sleep. Ginger tea is known to alleviate nausea and vomiting, and is also effective in reducing inflammation. For dehydration, rehydrating herbal teas with electrolytes, such as those containing lemon or lime, can help restore balance in the body. Licorice root tea can help with adrenal fatigue and stress-related symptoms. However, it is important to consult with a healthcare professional before using herbal teas as a treatment for any ailment.
